zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard ap Howell於1402年出生在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根時，他的父親Howell Ap Grono Vachan22歲，母親Joan ap Evan23歲。他與Alice Ap John結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard ap Howell于1402年出生在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根时，他的父亲Howell Ap Grono Vachan22岁，母亲Joan ap Evan23岁。他与Alice Ap John结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: from a short form of the personal name Adelhard, which is of ancient Germanic origin, composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + hard ‘bold, hardy’. Compare Ale and Alma 4.
Swedish: variant of   Ahl  .

variants.name: Ale
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos Enrique, Cesar, Jose, Maria De Jesus.
Spanish (Andalusia; Alé): unexplained.
Italian (Sicily; Alé): probably a variant of Aleo .

nameMeanings.name: Howell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh and English: in the Welsh border counties, from the Welsh male personal name Hywel ‘eminent’, popular since the Middle Ages particularly in honor of the great 10th-century law-giving Welsh king. The name may also arise from the Breton personal name Houuel, Huwel, Huwal, Howael, cognate with the Welsh name. Bretons were especially numerous in the eastern counties of England. See Powell .
English: perhaps from a Middle English personal name Huwel (spelled Howel), a shortening of Huwelot or Huelin (see Hewlett and Huling ), both pet forms of Middle English Huw (see Hugh ).
English: habitational name from Howell (Lincolnshire), from an uncertain initial element + Old English wella ‘well, spring, stream’.

variants.name: Howle
meanings: English and Welsh:
variant of   Howell  .
variant of   Hole  .

variants.name: Howells
meanings: English and Welsh (Glamorgan): variant of Howell , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent English -s.

English: perhaps occasionally a nickname for someone who was fearless, from Middle English houe, houwe ‘anxiety, fear’ (Old English hogu or hoga) + les ‘less’. If this has survived as a modern surname, its pronunciation has been influenced by that of the name in 1 above.
English: variant of   Holes  .

variants.name: Powell
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Hywel ‘son of Hywel’, a personal name meaning ‘eminent’ (see Howell ).
Irish: mainly of Welsh origin as in 1 above, but sometimes a surname adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Mac Giolla Phóil ‘son of the servant of Saint Paul’ (see Guilfoyle ).
English: variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard ap Howell was born in 1402, in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Howell Ap Grono Vachan, was 22 and his mother, Joan ap Evan, was 23. He married Alice Ap John. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard ap Howell est né en 1402, à Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Howell Ap Grono Vachan, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Joan ap Evan, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Alice Ap John. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard ap Howell 1402 in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Howell Ap Grono Vachan 22 und seine Mutter Joan ap Evan 23. Er hat Alice Ap John geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard ap Howell(이)가 1402년 영국 웨일스 글램르건에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Howell Ap Grono Vachan(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan ap Evan(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Alice Ap John(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard ap Howell родился в 1402 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Howell Ap Grono Vachan было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Joan ap Evan, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Ap John. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.
